data.description:
# Overlay triggering: deterministic-where-low-FP, LLM-router otherwise

## Why
Running every overlay on every plan is wasteful and noisy; the router decides relevance. Some overlays have a reliable deterministic trigger (keyword/signal) and should fire that way to avoid an LLM call; the rest need a judgment pass.

## What
A two-path overlay trigger: a deterministic detector where a low-FP signal exists, with an LLM relevance pass as the fallback/for ambiguous cases.

## Scope
- Overlays: T1 prior-art/novel-arch, T5a perf, T5b reliability, T5c security, T5d accessibility, T5e maintainability, T6 UX, T7 docs, T8 LLM-antipatterns, T9 shared-state.
- Deterministic triggers (low-FP keyword/signal): e.g. T5d a11y <- UI-keyword count; T5c security <- endpoint/auth/PII/credential/token; T1 <- the 6 bright-lines (external-integration/unfamiliar-dep/security-auth/novel-pattern/perf-scalability/migration).
- LLM-router relevance pass for overlays without a low-FP deterministic signal, and to confirm ambiguous deterministic hits.
- Calibration: track each overlay's downstream dismissal rate (over/under-triggering) per the observability substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A deterministic trigger per overlay that has a low-FP signal, unit-tested for precision on fixtures.
- [ ] An LLM relevance pass covering the remaining overlays + ambiguous deterministic hits.
- [ ] Each overlay descriptor declares whether it triggers deterministically or by LLM, in the registry.
- [ ] Overlay fire/dismissal is recorded in REVIEW_RESULT for offline calibration.
data.title: Overlay triggering: deterministic-where-low-FP, LLM-router otherwise
